back: One system that combines a data lake's cheap, open storage with a data warehouse's reliability and structure.
Running both separately means copying data, drift, double cost, and silos. One system removes that friction.
Standard, non-proprietary file formats that keep data accessible to many tools and under your control.
One trusted, governed copy of the data that everyone reads — so reports finally agree.
Organizing the lakehouse into bronze, silver, and gold layers that progressively refine data quality.
Raw data kept exactly as it arrived, preserving full fidelity so it can be reprocessed later.
Cleaned, validated, deduplicated, and joined data — the trustworthy, detailed foundation.
Business-ready aggregated tables and metrics, shaped for dashboards, reports, and decisions.
The lakehouse's unified governance layer: access control, data lineage, and data discovery in one place.
A record of where data came from and how it was transformed, from raw source to final report.
